snippet: private static final DecimalFormat MONEY = new DecimalFormat("#,##0.00");
description: MONEY is a shared static java.text.DecimalFormat, which is documented as NOT thread-safe. It is used from money() (line 84) via toPayload() (line 78), which the webhook dispatcher invokes for Discord deliveries. WebhookDispatcherService.deliverDue() fans the batch across a bounded fixed thread pool of deliveryConcurrency (default 8) threads (init(), line 111-119; invokeAll, line 200), and each deliver() calls DiscordWebhook.toPayload for a Discord target (line 230). Concurrent format() calls on one DecimalFormat instance can interleave the internal DigitList/FieldPosition state, producing garbled amount strings (e.g. mixed-up digits) or an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException — corrupting the money value shown in a delivered Discord embed, or failing the delivery outright.
recommendation: Do not share a mutable DecimalFormat across threads. Either construct a fresh DecimalFormat inside money() per call, wrap it in a ThreadLocal<DecimalFormat>, or format with a thread-safe alternative (e.g. String.format("%,.2f", amount.abs()) or a java.util.Formatter local to the call). Since money() already builds a small string, a per-call formatter is simplest and cheap relative to the outbound HTTP POST.
verification_note: Snippet confirmed verbatim at DiscordWebhook.java:37 (private static final DecimalFormat MONEY = new DecimalFormat("#,##0.00")). The concurrency chain also confirmed: WebhookDispatcherService.init() (line 119) builds a fixed thread pool of deliveryConcurrency threads; deliverDue() invokeAll's the batch across it (line 200); each deliver() calls DiscordWebhook.toPayload(d) (line 230), which reaches money() -> MONEY.format() (DiscordWebhook.java:84). java.text.DecimalFormat is documented as not synchronized, so concurrent format() calls on the one shared static instance can interleave internal DigitList state (garbled digits) or throw AIOOBE. Defect is real and easily triggered whenever a batch contains 2+ Discord deliveries. However major overstates the impact: the corrupted value lives only in a transient Discord embed notification (never persisted to the ledger, which is written solely by the DB trigger), and deliver() is explicitly designed never to throw out (invokeAll just waits; a format() exception fails only that one delivery, not the batch/loop per the ADT-116/117 comments at lines 188-223). Blast radius is a cosmetic notification for one delivery in a game economy, not money movement or data integrity. Fair severity is minor.

description: The 1-based-page → int-offset conversion with overflow guard is duplicated verbatim between ChestShopService.validateOffset (lines 161-172) and TransactionService.getTransactions (lines 85-91). Both compute (long)(page-1)*limit, reject offsets > Integer.MAX_VALUE with the same 'page is too large' 400, and cast down to int. The totalPages Math.ceil computation (ChestShopService line 222, TransactionService line 100) is likewise duplicated. This is the pagination arithmetic the whole API depends on; a change to the cap or message in one place silently diverges from the other.
recommendation: Extract the offset-overflow computation and totalPages into one shared helper (e.g. a small Pagination utility in a common location for the module) and call it from both services so the bound and error contract live in one place.

description: The byte[] → lowercase-hex encoding loop using Character.forDigit((b >> 4) & 0xF, 16)/(b & 0xF) is copy-pasted between WebhookService.newSecret (line 133) and HmacSha256.hex (lines 27-31). TransferService.sha256Hex (lines 494-498) is a third hex encoder using a slightly different idiom (String.format("%02x")). Three hand-rolled hex encoders for the same operation invite drift and are individually re-implementing a stdlib-level utility.
recommendation: Consolidate to a single hex helper (e.g. java.util.HexFormat.of() from the JDK, which returns lowercase hex directly) and call it from newSecret, HmacSha256.hex, and TransferService.sha256Hex.

description: Dangling orphan Javadoc comment at the end of FirmService with no method following it. The empty-to-null helper it once documented was moved to FirmFieldLimits.emptyToNull (ADT-120, referenced at line 37), but the comment was left behind attached to the class's closing brace, where it documents nothing.
recommendation: Delete the stray comment on lines 228-229.
